import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut has_num = false
  mut neg = false
  mut val = 0
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b >= 48 and b <= 57
        val = val * 10 + (b - 48)
        has_num = true
      else if b == 45
        if has_num
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, -val)
          else
            result = list.push(result, val)
          end
        end
        val = 0
        neg = true
        has_num = true
      else
        if has_num
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, -val)
          else
            result = list.push(result, val)
          end
          val = 0
          neg = false
          has_num = false
        end
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has_num
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, -val)
    else
      result = list.push(result, val)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ arr: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = arr.len() - 1
  while lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    let v = arr[mid]
    if v == t
      return Some(mid)
    else if v < t
      lo = mid + 1
    else
      hi = mid - 1
    end
  end
  None
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(line1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line2)
    let arr = parse_ints(line1)
    let nums = parse_ints(line2)
    match nums.get(0)
    when Some(t)
      match bsearch(arr, t)
      when Some(idx)
        print("index=#{idx}")
      when None
        print("not_found")
      end
    when None
      print("not_found")
    end
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end
when None
  print("not_found")
end
